title: luma anceps
intro: Luma anceps, also known as Chilean Boxwood, is a small, evergreen shrub native to the Andes mountains in South America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, Luma anceps is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
